---
title: "KY_2021_Tornado"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-23"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

##Packages
```{r message=FALSE}

library(conflicted)

# Set preferences for all conflicts explicitly
conflict_prefer("filter", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("select", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("between", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("first", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("last", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("lag", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("lead", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("expand", "tidyr")
conflict_prefer("pack", "tidyr")
conflict_prefer("unpack", "tidyr")
conflict_prefer("year", "lubridate")
conflict_prefer("month", "lubridate")
conflict_prefer("week", "lubridate")
conflict_prefer("hour", "lubridate")
conflict_prefer("minute", "lubridate")
conflict_prefer("second", "lubridate")
conflict_prefer("mday", "lubridate")
conflict_prefer("wday", "lubridate")
conflict_prefer("yday", "lubridate")
conflict_prefer("isoweek", "lubridate")
conflict_prefer("isoyear", "lubridate")
conflict_prefer("quarter", "lubridate")
conflict_prefer("transpose", "purrr")
conflict_prefer("compose", "purrr")
conflict_prefer("continuous_summary", "gtsummary")
conflict_prefer("get_estimates", "tidycensus")

library(tidyverse)
library(foreign)
library(broom)
library(readxl)
library(plm)
library(tidycensus)
library(MatchIt)
library(countyhealthR)
library(data.table)
library(glmnet)
library(lmtest)
library(sandwich)
library(fixest)
library(modelsummary)
library(tinytable)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(gtsummary)
library(flextable)

```

##Data Load

```{r}

ky_tnd_dat <- readRDS("C:/Users/Custom/Dropbox/Post Doctoral Work/Pittsburgh_MCH/Projects/Kentucky_Tornado_2021_Disaster/Data/ky_tornado_21_data.rds")

#Add Tornado "Treatment Variable" for matching
ky_tnd_dat <- ky_tnd_dat %>%
  mutate(tornado = if_else(county == "Graves", 1,
                    if_else(county == "Fulton", 1,
                      if_else(county == "Hickman", 1,
                       if_else(county == "Marshall", 1,
                        if_else(county == "Lyon", 1, 
                          if_else(county == "Caldwell", 1,
                            if_else(county == "Hopkins", 1,
                              if_else(county == "Muhlenberg", 1, 
                                if_else(county == "Ohio", 1, 
                                  if_else(county == "Breckinridge", 1, 
                                    if_else(county == "Grayson", 1, 0)      
                              )      
                              )      
                              )      
                              )      
                              )      
                              )       
                              )        
                              )      
                              )       
                             ))
#Make Treatment a factor
ky_tnd_dat$tornado <- as.factor(ky_tnd_dat$tornado)

#dput(names(ky_tnd_dat)) #List headers with ""

#Selection
dat <- ky_tnd_dat %>% select("county", "year", "tornado", "percent_foodinsecure", "percent_poverty", 
"avg_number_snap", "avg_number_wic", contains("raw"))


dat$percent_poverty <- as.numeric(dat$percent_poverty)
dat$percent_foodinsecure <- as.numeric(dat$percent_foodinsecure)
dat$avg_number_snap <- as.numeric(dat$avg_number_snap)
dat$avg_number_wic <- as.numeric(dat$avg_number_wic)

```

Table 1
```{r}

# Step 1 — Convert proportions to percentages
dat <- dat %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    percent_foodinsecure                                        = percent_foodinsecure * 100,
    `unemployment raw value`                                    = `unemployment raw value` * 100,
    `children eligible for free or reduced price lunch raw value` = `children eligible for free or reduced price lunch raw value` * 100,
    `long commute - driving alone raw value`                    = `long commute - driving alone raw value` * 100,
    `children in single-parent households raw value`            = `children in single-parent households raw value` * 100,
    `disconnected youth raw value`                              = `disconnected youth raw value` * 100,
    `poor or fair health raw value`                             = `poor or fair health raw value` * 100,
    `excessive drinking raw value`                              = `excessive drinking raw value` * 100,
    `frequent physical distress raw value`                      = `frequent physical distress raw value` * 100,
    `severe housing problems raw value`                         = `severe housing problems raw value` * 100,
    `limited access to healthy foods raw value`                 = `limited access to healthy foods raw value` * 100
  )

# Step 2 — Recreate period variable
dat$period <- ifelse(dat$year %in% c(2018, 2019, 2020), "pre",
              ifelse(dat$year %in% c(2022, 2023), "post", NA_character_))

# Step 3 — Subset
df_pre  <- dat[dat$period == "pre" & !is.na(dat$period), ]
df_post <- dat[dat$period == "post" & !is.na(dat$period), ]

vars <- c("tornado", "percent_poverty", "percent_foodinsecure",
          "unemployment raw value", "income inequality raw value",
          "median household income raw value",
          "children eligible for free or reduced price lunch raw value",
          "long commute - driving alone raw value",
          "children in single-parent households raw value",
          "disconnected youth raw value", "poor or fair health raw value",
          "excessive drinking raw value", "frequent physical distress raw value",
          "severe housing problems raw value",
          "limited access to healthy foods raw value")

df_pre  <- df_pre[, vars]
df_post <- df_post[, vars]

# Step 4 — Labels
var_labels <- list(
  percent_poverty                                                 ~ "Children in Poverty (%)",
  percent_foodinsecure                                            ~ "Food Insecure Children (%)",
  `unemployment raw value`                                        ~ "Unemployment Rate (%)",
  `income inequality raw value`                                   ~ "Income Inequality (Ratio)",
  `median household income raw value`                             ~ "Median Household Income ($)",
  `children eligible for free or reduced price lunch raw value`   ~ "Free/Reduced Lunch Eligible (%)",
  `long commute - driving alone raw value`                        ~ "Long Commute — Driving Alone (%)",
  `children in single-parent households raw value`                ~ "Single-Parent Households (%)",
  `disconnected youth raw value`                                  ~ "Disconnected Youth (%)",
  `poor or fair health raw value`                                 ~ "Poor or Fair Health (%)",
  `excessive drinking raw value`                                  ~ "Excessive Drinking (%)",
  `frequent physical distress raw value`                          ~ "Frequent Physical Distress (%)",
  `severe housing problems raw value`                             ~ "Severe Housing Problems (%)",
  `limited access to healthy foods raw value`                     ~ "Limited Access to Healthy Foods (%)"
)

# Step 5 — Build tables
pre_table <- tbl_summary(
  df_pre,
  by = tornado,
  label = var_labels,
  statistic = all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
  digits = all_continuous() ~ 2,
  missing = "no"
) %>%
  modify_header(
    stat_1 ~ "**Control Pre**",
    stat_2 ~ "**Tornado Pre**"
  )

post_table <- tbl_summary(
  df_post,
  by = tornado,
  label = var_labels,
  statistic = all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
  digits = all_continuous() ~ 2,
  missing = "no"
) %>%
  modify_header(
    stat_1 ~ "**Control Post**",
    stat_2 ~ "**Tornado Post**"
  )

# Step 6 — Merge
tbl_merge(
  list(pre_table, post_table),
  tab_spanner = c("**Pre-Event (2018–2020)**", "**Post-Event (2022–2023)**")
)%>%
  as_flex_table() %>%
  flextable::save_as_docx(path = "C:/Users/Custom/Dropbox/Post Doctoral Work/Pittsburgh_MCH/Projects/Kentucky_Tornado_2021_Disaster/Data/table1_prepost.docx")




```

#Lasso Child Poverty
```{r}
#Run Lasso on Pre-Event Years Only

dat$year <- as.numeric(dat$year)
#1: 2018, 2:2019. 3:2020
dat <- dat %>% filter(year < 4) %>% select(-year)


# Define response variable as a vector
y <- dat$percent_poverty

# Handle NAs manually before matrix conversion
pre_data_clean <- dat %>%
  select(-county, -percent_poverty, -'children in poverty raw value', -`food insecurity raw value`, -percent_foodinsecure) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ifelse(is.na(.), mean(., na.rm = TRUE), .)))

# Convert to matrix directly
x_matrix <- as.matrix(pre_data_clean)

# Run cross-validated LASSO to find optimal lambda
lasso_cv <- cv.glmnet(x_matrix, y, alpha = 1)  # alpha = 1 is LASSO

# Plot cross-validation curve
plot(lasso_cv)

# Optimal lambda
best_lambda <- lasso_cv$lambda.min  # or lambda.1se for more regularization

# Extract selected coefficients
lasso_coefs <- coef(lasso_cv, s = "lambda.min")
print(lasso_coefs)

# Check how many variables survive at different lambda values
print(lasso_cv)


# Extract non-zero variables (selected covariates)
selected_vars <- rownames(lasso_coefs)[lasso_coefs[,1] != 0]
selected_vars  # these go into your event study model


# Try lambda.1se instead of lambda.min
lasso_coefs_1se <- coef(lasso_cv, s = "lambda.1se")
print(lasso_coefs_1se)

# Extract non-zero variables (selected covariates)
selected_vars <- rownames(lasso_coefs_1se)[lasso_coefs_1se[,1] != 0]
selected_vars  # these go into your event study model

```

Hausman Test
```{r}


dat <- ky_tnd_dat %>% select("county", "year", "tornado", "percent_foodinsecure", "percent_poverty", 
"avg_number_snap", "avg_number_wic", contains("raw"))


dat$percent_poverty <- as.numeric(dat$percent_poverty)
dat$percent_foodinsecure <- as.numeric(dat$percent_foodinsecure)
dat$avg_number_snap <- as.numeric(dat$avg_number_snap)
dat$avg_number_wic <- as.numeric(dat$avg_number_wic)

# Set up panel data structure
panel_data <- pdata.frame(dat, index = c("county", "year"))

#dput(names(panel_data))

# Run fixed effects model
fe_model <- plm(percent_poverty ~ year + tornado + poor.or.fair.health.raw.value  +  excessive.drinking.raw.value  +  unemployment.raw.value  + income.inequality.raw.value  +  children.in.single.parent.households.raw.value  +  severe.housing.problems.raw.value  +  long.commute...driving.alone.raw.value  +  frequent.physical.distress.raw.value  + limited.access.to.healthy.foods.raw.value + disconnected.youth.raw.value + median.household.income.raw.value + children.in.single.parent.households.raw.value, 
                data = panel_data, 
                model = "within")

# Run random effects model
re_model <- plm(percent_poverty ~ year + tornado + poor.or.fair.health.raw.value  +  excessive.drinking.raw.value  +  unemployment.raw.value  + income.inequality.raw.value  +  children.in.single.parent.households.raw.value  +  severe.housing.problems.raw.value  +  long.commute...driving.alone.raw.value  +  frequent.physical.distress.raw.value  + limited.access.to.healthy.foods.raw.value + disconnected.youth.raw.value + median.household.income.raw.value + children.in.single.parent.households.raw.value, 
                data = panel_data, 
                model = "random")

# Hausman test
hausman_test <- phtest(fe_model, re_model)
print(hausman_test)

``` 



```{r}

# Create relative time indicators
dat_rel <- dat %>%
  mutate(year = as.numeric(as.character(year)),
         rel_time = year - 2021) # 2021 = 0, 2018 = -3, 2023 = +2

# Work on original data BEFORE pdata.frame()
# First check what values tornado actually has
unique(dat_rel$tornado)
levels(dat_rel$tornado)

dat_rel <- dat_rel %>%
  mutate(
    treated_km3 = ifelse(tornado == 1 & rel_time == -3, 1, 0),
    treated_km2 = ifelse(tornado == 1 & rel_time == -2, 1, 0),
    treated_km1 = ifelse(tornado == 1 & rel_time == -1, 1, 0),
    treated_kp1 = ifelse(tornado == 1 & rel_time ==  1, 1, 0),
    treated_kp2 = ifelse(tornado == 1 & rel_time ==  2, 1, 0)
  )


# Verify
dat_rel %>%
  summarise(
    km3 = sum(treated_km3),
    km2 = sum(treated_km2),
    km1 = sum(treated_km1),
    kp1 = sum(treated_kp1),
    kp2 = sum(treated_kp2)
  )


# Set up panel
panel_data <- pdata.frame(dat_rel, index = c("county", "year"))


es_poverty <- feols(percent_poverty ~ 
                      treated_km3 + treated_km2 + treated_km1 +
                      treated_kp1 + treated_kp2 +
                      poor.or.fair.health.raw.value +
                      excessive.drinking.raw.value +
                      unemployment.raw.value +
                      income.inequality.raw.value +
                      children.in.single.parent.households.raw.value +
                      severe.housing.problems.raw.value +
                      long.commute...driving.alone.raw.value +
                      frequent.physical.distress.raw.value +
                      limited.access.to.healthy.foods.raw.value +
                      disconnected.youth.raw.value +
                      median.household.income.raw.value |
                      county + year,  # fixed effects go here
                    cluster = ~county,
                    data = panel_data)

summary(es_poverty)

```


##Lasso Food Insecurity

```{r}
#Run Lasso on Pre-Event Years Only

dat$year <- as.numeric(dat$year)
#1: 2018, 2:2019. 3:2020
dat <- dat %>% filter(year < 4) %>% select(-year)


# Define response variable as a vector
y <- dat$percent_foodinsecure

# Handle NAs manually before matrix conversion
pre_data_clean <- dat %>%
  select(-county, -percent_poverty, -'children in poverty raw value', -`food insecurity raw value`, -percent_foodinsecure, -tornado) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ifelse(is.na(.), mean(., na.rm = TRUE), .)))

# Convert to matrix directly
x_matrix <- as.matrix(pre_data_clean)

# Run cross-validated LASSO to find optimal lambda
lasso_cv <- cv.glmnet(x_matrix, y, alpha = 1)  # alpha = 1 is LASSO

# Plot cross-validation curve
plot(lasso_cv)

# Optimal lambda
best_lambda <- lasso_cv$lambda.min  # or lambda.1se for more regularization

# Extract selected coefficients
lasso_coefs <- coef(lasso_cv, s = "lambda.min")
print(lasso_coefs)

# Check how many variables survive at different lambda values
print(lasso_cv)


# Extract non-zero variables (selected covariates)
selected_vars <- rownames(lasso_coefs)[lasso_coefs[,1] != 0]
selected_vars  # these go into your event study model


# Try lambda.1se instead of lambda.min
lasso_coefs_1se <- coef(lasso_cv, s = "lambda.1se")
print(lasso_coefs_1se)

# Extract non-zero variables (selected covariates)
selected_vars <- rownames(lasso_coefs_1se)[lasso_coefs_1se[,1] != 0]
selected_vars  # these go into your event study model

```

Hausman Test
```{r}

# Set up panel data structure
panel_data <- pdata.frame(dat, index = c("county", "year"))

dput(names(panel_data))

# Run fixed effects model
fe_model <- plm(percent_foodinsecure ~ year + tornado + 
                    adult.smoking.raw.value +
                    food.environment.index.raw.value +
                    excessive.drinking.raw.value +
                    unemployment.raw.value +
                    children.in.single.parent.households.raw.value +
                    severe.housing.problems.raw.value +
                    median.household.income.raw.value +
                    high.school.graduation.raw.value +
                    driving.alone.to.work.raw.value, 
                data = panel_data, 
                model = "within")

# Run random effects model: Fails due to collinearity issues


# Hausman test
#hausman_test <- phtest(fe_model, re_model)
#print(hausman_test)

#fe_model <- plm(percent_poverty ~ tornado + factor(year) +
 #               unemployment_raw_value +
  #              income_inequality_raw_value +
   #             # ... rest of your covariates
    #            data = panel_data,
     #           model = "within")

``` 



```{r}


dat <- ky_tnd_dat %>% select("county", "year", "tornado", "percent_foodinsecure", "percent_poverty", 
"avg_number_snap", "avg_number_wic", contains("raw"))


dat$percent_poverty <- as.numeric(dat$percent_poverty)
dat$percent_foodinsecure <- as.numeric(dat$percent_foodinsecure)
dat$avg_number_snap <- as.numeric(dat$avg_number_snap)
dat$avg_number_wic <- as.numeric(dat$avg_number_wic)


# Create relative time indicators
dat_rel <- dat %>%
  mutate(year = as.numeric(as.character(year)),
         rel_time = year - 2021) # 2021 = 0, 2018 = -3, 2023 = +2

# Work on original data BEFORE pdata.frame()
# First check what values tornado actually has
unique(dat_rel$tornado)
levels(dat_rel$tornado)

dat_rel <- dat_rel %>%
  mutate(
    treated_km3 = ifelse(tornado == 1 & rel_time == -3, 1, 0),
    treated_km2 = ifelse(tornado == 1 & rel_time == -2, 1, 0),
    treated_km1 = ifelse(tornado == 1 & rel_time == -1, 1, 0),
    treated_kp1 = ifelse(tornado == 1 & rel_time ==  1, 1, 0),
    treated_kp2 = ifelse(tornado == 1 & rel_time ==  2, 1, 0)
  )


# Verify
dat_rel %>%
  summarise(
    km3 = sum(treated_km3),
    km2 = sum(treated_km2),
    km1 = sum(treated_km1),
    kp1 = sum(treated_kp1),
    kp2 = sum(treated_kp2)
  )


# Set up panel
panel_data <- pdata.frame(dat_rel, index = c("county", "year"))


es_fsecure <- feols(percent_foodinsecure ~ 
                      treated_km3 + treated_km2 + treated_km1 +
                      treated_kp1 + treated_kp2 +
                      adult.smoking.raw.value +
                      food.environment.index.raw.value +
                      excessive.drinking.raw.value +
                      unemployment.raw.value +
                      children.in.single.parent.households.raw.value +
                      severe.housing.problems.raw.value +
                      median.household.income.raw.value +
                      high.school.graduation.raw.value +
                      driving.alone.to.work.raw.value|
                      county + year,  # fixed effects go here
                    cluster = ~county,
                    data = panel_data)

summary(es_fsecure)

```


#Matched County Love Plot

```{r}
library(cobalt)

# Create the love plot
love.plot(
  x = tornado ~ 
    percent_poverty +
    percent_foodinsecure +
    `unemployment raw value` +
    `income inequality raw value` +
    `median household income raw value` +
    `children eligible for free or reduced price lunch raw value` +
    `long commute - driving alone raw value` +
    `children in single-parent households raw value` +
    `disconnected youth raw value` +
    `poor or fair health raw value` +
    `excessive drinking raw value` +
    `frequent physical distress raw value` +
    `severe housing problems raw value` +
    `limited access to healthy foods raw value`,
  data = dat %>% filter(year == 2019),
  threshold = 0.1,          # reference line at SMD 0.1
  var.order = "unadjusted", # order by SMD size
  abs = TRUE,               # absolute SMD
  title = "Covariate Balance: Tornado-Affected vs. Matched Control Counties (2019)",
  labels = TRUE
)

```




Table 2
```{r}

#Define pre and post periods
df <- dat %>%
  mutate(period = case_when(
    year %in% c(2018, 2019, 2020) ~ "pre",
    year %in% c(2022, 2023) ~ "post",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )) %>%
  filter(!is.na(period))

# Function to calculate mean and SD
mean_sd <- function(x) {
  paste0(round(mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), 2), 
         " (", round(sd(x, na.rm = TRUE), 2), ")")
}

# Generate summary table
table2 <- df %>%
  group_by(storm, period) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n_distinct(county),
    children_poverty     = mean_sd(children_poverty),
    food_insecure        = mean_sd(food_insecure),
    unemployment         = mean_sd(unemployment),
    income_inequality    = mean_sd(income_inequality),
    median_hh_income     = mean_sd(median_hh_income),
    free_lunch           = mean_sd(free_lunch),
    long_commute         = mean_sd(long_commute),
    single_parent        = mean_sd(single_parent),
    disconnected_youth   = mean_sd(disconnected_youth),
    poor_fair_health     = mean_sd(poor_fair_health),
    excessive_drinking   = mean_sd(excessive_drinking),
    freq_physical_dist   = mean_sd(freq_physical_dist),
    severe_housing       = mean_sd(severe_housing),
    limited_food_access  = mean_sd(limited_food_access),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(group = case_when(
    storm == 1 & period == "pre"  ~ "Tornado Pre",
    storm == 1 & period == "post" ~ "Tornado Post",
    storm == 0 & period == "pre"  ~ "Control Pre",
    storm == 0 & period == "post" ~ "Control Post"
  )) %>%
  select(group, everything(), -storm, -period)

# Pivot to wide format for table
table2_wide <- table2 %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -group, names_to = "characteristic", values_to = "value") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = group, values_from = value)

# View
print(table2_wide)

# Export to CSV for reference
#write_csv(table2_wide, "table2_pre_post_summary.csv")


```



Pooled Regression Tables
```{r}

# Create named list of models
models <- list(
  "Child Poverty" = es_poverty,
  "Food Insecurity" = es_fsecure
)

# Clean coefficient names for display
coef_map <- c(
  "treated_km3" = "2018 (k-3)",
  "treated_km2" = "2019 (k-2)",
  "treated_km1" = "2020 (k-1, COVID)",
  "treated_kp1" = "2022 (k+1)",
  "treated_kp2" = "2023 (k+2)",
  "poor or fair health raw value" = "Poor/Fair Health",
  "excessive drinking raw value" = "Excessive Drinking",
  "unemployment raw value" = "Unemployment",
  "income inequality raw value" = "Income Inequality",
  "children in single-parent households raw value" = "Single-Parent Households",
  "severe housing problems raw value" = "Severe Housing Problems",
  "long commute - driving alone raw value" = "Long Commute (Driving Alone)",
  "frequent physical distress raw value" = "Frequent Physical Distress",
  "limited access to healthy foods raw value" = "Limited Food Access",
  "disconnected youth raw value" = "Disconnected Youth",
  "median household income raw value" = "Median Household Income",
  "children eligible for free or reduced price lunch raw value" = "Free/Reduced Lunch Eligible",
  "adult smoking raw value" = "Adult Smoking",
  "food environment index raw value" = "Food Environment Index",
  "high school graduation raw value" = "High School Graduation",
  "driving alone to work raw value" = "Driving Alone to Work"
)

# Generate table
modelsummary(
  models,
  coef_map = coef_map,
  stars = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  gof_map = c("nobs", "r.squared", "adj.r.squared"),
  title = "Event Study Results: Child Poverty and Food Insecurity Following the 2021 Kentucky Tornado Outbreak",
  notes = "Fixed effects for county and year. Standard errors clustered at county level. Reference period: 2021 (event year).",
  output = "C:/Users/Custom/Dropbox/Post Doctoral Work/Pittsburgh_MCH/Kentucky_Tornado_2021_Disaster/Data/tornado_cross_table.docx"  # saves directly to Word
)

```

Event Plot

Children in Poverty
```{r}


# Child poverty event study results
poverty_es <- data.frame(
  period = c(-3, -2, -1, 1, 2),
  estimate = c(-0.5763, 4.0634, 2.7963, -2.1405, -0.3313),
  se = c(3.0624, 2.4685, 2.9149, 5.0065, 4.9698)
) %>%
  mutate(
    ci_low  = estimate - 1.96 * se,
    ci_high = estimate + 1.96 * se,
    period_label = as.character(c("k-3", "k-2", "k-1", "k+1", "k+2")),
    post = ifelse(period > 0, "Post-Event", "Pre-Event")
  )

# Add reference period k=0
poverty_es <- poverty_es %>%
  bind_rows(
    data.frame(
      period = 0, estimate = 0, se = 0,
      ci_low = 0, ci_high = 0,
      period_label = "k0", post = "Reference"
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    period_label = factor(period_label,
                          levels = c("k-3","k-2","k-1","k0","k+1","k+2")),
    post = factor(post, levels = c("Pre-Event","Reference","Post-Event"))
  )

# Plot
ggplot(poverty_es, aes(x = period_label, y = estimate, color = post, shape = post)) +

  # Reference lines
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 3.5, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.5) +

  # Confidence intervals
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high),
                width = 0.15, linewidth = 0.6) +

  # Point estimates
  geom_point(size = 3) +

  # Colors and shapes — matching food insecurity scheme
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Pre-Event" = "#2E5090", "Reference" = "grey40", "Post-Event" = "#0F6E56"),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c("Pre-Event" = 16, "Reference" = 1, "Post-Event" = 16),
    name = NULL
  ) +

  # Annotation
  annotate("text", x = 3.5, y = max(poverty_es$ci_high) + 1.5,
           label = "Tornado\noutbreak", size = 3, color = "grey40",
           hjust = 0.5, lineheight = 0.9) +

  # Labels
  labs(
    title = "Event Study: Effect of 2021 Tornado Outbreak on Children in Poverty",
    subtitle = "Tornado-affected vs. matched control counties, Kentucky 2018–2023",
    x = "Period relative to tornado outbreak (k)",
    y = "Estimated effect on children in poverty (%)",
    caption = "Note: Estimates from two-way fixed effects model with county and year fixed effects.\nReference period k = −1 omitted. Error bars represent 95% confidence intervals.\nStandard errors clustered at county level."
  ) +

  # Theme — matching food insecurity
  theme_minimal(base_family = "serif", base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 10),
    plot.caption  = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 8, hjust = 0),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.4)
  )

# Save
ggsave("C:/Users/Custom/Dropbox/Post Doctoral Work/Pittsburgh_MCH/Projects/Kentucky_Tornado_2021_Disaster/Data/event_study_poverty.png", width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
ggsave("C:/Users/Custom/Dropbox/Post Doctoral Work/Pittsburgh_MCH/Projects/Kentucky_Tornado_2021_Disaster/Data/event_study_poverty.pdf", width = 7, height = 5)

```


```{r}

# Food insecurity event study results
food_insecurity_es <- data.frame(
  period = c(-3, -2, -1, 1, 2),
  estimate = c(-0.010765127, -0.004972096, 0.004941898, 0.000496537, 0.000638817),
  se = c(0.011193929, 0.009530365, 0.007501369, 0.007108415, 0.007287591)
) %>%
  mutate(
    ci_low  = estimate - 1.96 * se,
    ci_high = estimate + 1.96 * se,
    period_label = factor(period, 
                          levels = c(-3, -2, -1, 1, 2),
                          labels = c("k-3", "k-2", "k-1", "k+1", "k+2")),
    post = ifelse(period > 0, "Post-Event", "Pre-Event")
  )

# Add reference period k=0 (omitted, estimate = 0)
reference <- data.frame(
  period = 0,
  estimate = 0,
  se = 0,
  ci_low = 0,
  ci_high = 0,
  period_label = factor("k0", levels = c("k-3","k-2","k-1","k0","k+1","k+2")),
  post = "Reference"
)

# Rebuild with reference included
food_insecurity_es <- food_insecurity_es %>%
  mutate(period_label = as.character(period_label)) %>%
  bind_rows(
    data.frame(
      period = 0, estimate = 0, se = 0,
      ci_low = 0, ci_high = 0,
      period_label = "k0", post = "Reference"
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    period_label = factor(period_label, 
                          levels = c("k-3","k-2","k-1","k0","k+1","k+2")),
    post = factor(post, levels = c("Pre-Event","Reference","Post-Event"))
  )

# Plot
ggplot(food_insecurity_es, aes(x = period_label, y = estimate, color = post, shape = post)) +
  
  # Reference lines
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 3.5, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.5) +
  
  # Confidence intervals
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high),
                width = 0.15, linewidth = 0.6) +
  
  # Point estimates
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  
  # Colors and shapes
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Pre-Event" = "#2E5090", "Reference" = "grey40", "Post-Event" = "#0F6E56"),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c("Pre-Event" = 16, "Reference" = 1, "Post-Event" = 16),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  
  # Annotation
  annotate("text", x = 3.5, y = max(food_insecurity_es$ci_high) + 0.003,
           label = "Tornado\noutbreak", size = 3, color = "grey40",
           hjust = 0.5, lineheight = 0.9) +
  
  # Labels
  labs(
    title = "Event Study: Effect of 2021 Tornado Outbreak on Child Food Insecurity",
    subtitle = "Tornado-affected vs. matched control counties, Kentucky 2018–2023",
    x = "Period relative to tornado outbreak (k)",
    y = "Estimated effect on food insecurity rate",
    caption = "Note: Estimates from two-way fixed effects model with county and year fixed effects.\nReference period k = −1 omitted. Error bars represent 95% confidence intervals.\nStandard errors clustered at county level."
  ) +
  
  # Theme
  theme_minimal(base_family = "serif", base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 10),
    plot.caption  = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 8, hjust = 0),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.4)
  )

# Save


ggsave("C:/Users/Custom/Dropbox/Post Doctoral Work/Pittsburgh_MCH/Projects/Kentucky_Tornado_2021_Disaster/Data/event_study_food_insecurity.png", width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
ggsave("C:/Users/Custom/Dropbox/Post Doctoral Work/Pittsburgh_MCH/Projects/Kentucky_Tornado_2021_Disaster/Data/event_study_food_insecurity.pdf", width = 7, height = 5)

```
